title: Complete the COMMANDS.md CLI reference (config, telemetry, worktree, memory, facts)
description: COMMANDS.md opens by declaring itself the 'Complete reference for all bober CLI commands' (COMMANDS.md:2) but has no section for five registered, non-hidden top-level command groups. Add a documentation section for config, telemetry, worktree, memory, and facts so the claim holds against the real CLI in src/cli/index.ts.

successCriteria.description: COMMANDS.md contains a level-3 heading '### `bober config`' (or `bober config migrate`), '### `bober telemetry`', '### `bober worktree`', '### `bober memory`', and '### `bober facts`'. Verify: grep -cE "^### .*\`bober (config|telemetry|worktree|memory|facts)" COMMANDS.md returns at least 5.

Every top-level command registered in src/cli/index.ts and src/fleet/index.ts has a matching '### ' heading in COMMANDS.md — an outsider iterating the registered command names finds zero without a documentation heading (specifically config, telemetry, worktree, memory, facts are no longer missing).

Each new section documents that command's actual subcommands as registered: config migrate; telemetry status, telemetry purge, telemetry export; worktree run; memory distill, memory list, memory show, memory prune; facts add, facts list, facts show, facts invalidate. Verify each subcommand token appears under its command's heading and matches src/cli/commands/*.ts.

The new sections describe only flags and behavior present in the command source (src/cli/commands/{config,telemetry,worktree,memory,facts}.ts) — no invented flags, no fabricated behavior. Spot-check every documented flag against the corresponding .option()/.description() in source.
npm run build (tsc) exits 0 and git diff --name-only lists only COMMANDS.md — confirming the change is docs-only with no source or test edits.

nonGoals: Do not create, move, or push any git tags (v0.12.0-v0.18.0 already exist).
Do not modify any file under src/ or any *.test.ts file.
Do not add, rename, or change any CLI command registration.
Do not rewrite, reorder, or delete existing COMMANDS.md sections beyond inserting the five new command sections.
Do not document flags or subcommands that are not present in src/cli/commands/*.ts.
stopConditions: A completeness check confirms every top-level command registered in src/cli/index.ts and src/fleet/index.ts has a '### ' heading in COMMANDS.md (zero missing).
grep -cE "^### .*\`bober (config|telemetry|worktree|memory|facts)" COMMANDS.md returns at least 5, npm run build exits 0, and git diff --name-only shows only COMMANDS.md.
definitionOfDone: COMMANDS.md is once again a complete CLI reference: a reader can find documented sections for config, telemetry, worktree, memory, and facts (with their real subcommands) formatted like the neighboring sections, and a script iterating the commands registered in src/cli/index.ts plus src/fleet/index.ts finds no top-level command without a documentation heading. Only COMMANDS.md changed and the project still builds.
assumptions: config/telemetry/worktree/memory/facts are user-facing and non-hidden — registered in src/cli/index.ts:312-324 with .description() text; grep 'hidden' in each command file returns nothing (Q3).
COMMANDS.md:2 declaring itself the 'Complete reference for all bober CLI commands' makes the five missing sections genuine drift (spec assumption 1).

Subcommand lists are taken from the .command()/.description() registrations in src/cli/commands/{config,telemetry,worktree,memory,facts}.ts (config migrate; telemetry status/purge/export; worktree run; memory distill/list/show/prune; facts add/list/show/invalidate).
outOfScope: README.md and package.json changes (handled in Sprint 2).
VISION.md, docs/providers.md, CHANGELOG.md edits (spot-checked current / out of scope).
generatorNotes: Read each command's source before writing its section so descriptions match reality: src/cli/commands/config.ts (config: 'Inspect and migrate bober.config.json'; config migrate: 'Add all new schema fields with default values'), telemetry.ts (telemetry status / purge / export — opt-in, local-only), worktree.ts (worktree run <task> — launch/manage worktree-isolated pipeline runs), memory.ts (memory distill / list / show <lessonId> / prune — self-improvement lessons index), facts.ts (facts add / list / show <id> / invalidate <id> — semantic FactStore). Mirror the format of an existing section such as COMMANDS.md:801-871 (Graph & Utility Commands): a '### `bober <cmd>`' heading, a one-sentence purpose line, a fenced usage block, then flags/behavior. Place the new sections in a sensible spot (e.g., after 'Utility Commands' or grouped as a new '## Configuration & Introspection Commands' block) without disturbing existing sections. Do NOT touch any src/ file.
evaluatorNotes: Verify sc-1-2 by listing every top-level command from src/cli/index.ts (init, update, plan, sprint, eval, run, mcp, graph, onboard, impact, approve, reject, list-approvals, audit, rollback, postmortem, incident, playbook, config, telemetry, worktree, memory, facts, task, medical, research, calendar, vault, chat, blackboard, hub, do, telegram, security-audit) plus fleet from src/fleet/index.ts, and confirming each has a '### ' heading in COMMANDS.md — the five previously-missing ones (config, telemetry, worktree, memory, facts) must now be present. Confirm sc-1-4 by opening the five command source files and checking that no documented flag is absent from the source. Confirm docs-only via git diff --name-only (only COMMANDS.md) and npm run build exit 0.
